data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Surges Despite New Congressional Attack
data.description:
Today was a strange one for chip stock Intel (INTC). What should have been a disaster—or at least a cause for concern—was met with surprising acclaim. A bipartisan effort emerged from Congress and took aim at Intel, calling it out over issues of Chinese technology. Shareholders, however, seemed not even sort of bothered, as evidenced by the share price surge of nearly 6.5% seen in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Half a dozen lawmakers, both Republican and Democrat alike, sent a letter out Lip-Bu Tan's way, asking the current Intel CEO whether or not the use of ACM Research (ACMR) tools would create "…national security risks." Further, the letter asked if the use of such tools would "…potentially allow China to gain access to Intel's chip making processes."

Given that Intel is now partially taxpayer-owned, with 10% of the company in America's hands, it was a question worth asking. But Intel's response must have been sufficient to take any investor concerns out of the picture. Intel rep Sophie Won Metzger noted, "All Intel activities fully comply with applicable U.S laws and regulations, and we engage regularly with the U.S government on security matters." This is a bit boilerplate, but good enough for most to accept. Metzger then followed up with the fact that Intel had systems in place to "…limit the information any individual tool can receive during the manufacturing process."

Out With the Old

Intel also lost a major figure in its governance, as board chairman Frank Yeary plans to retire. Yeary will be replaced by Craig Barratt after Yeary steps down following the annual shareholder meeting this May.

This is regarded as a major shift for Intel, as Yeary has been chair of the board since 2023, and has served on the board since 2009. But Yeary had little more than kind words for Intel and new CEO Lip-Bu Tan. Seaport Global analyst Jay Goldberg noted, "I think his departure was long overdue. Intel has made a lot of bad decisions (during Yeary's tenure)…"

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 107.11% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 4.94% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Google (GOOGL) Gemini Causing People to Commit Suicide? A New Lawsuit Says 'Yes'
data.description:
Google's parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) has been hit with a new lawsuit that alleges the company's artificial intelligence chatbot Gemini caused a man to commit suicide.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The lawsuit against Alphabet alleges that Gemini guided 36-year-old Jonathan Gavalas to commit suicide. But that's not all. The legal action also says that Gemini encouraged Gavalas to stage a "catastrophic accident" near Miami International Airport as part of an escalating series of delusions.

The lawsuit says that the Gemini chatbot drove Gavalas to kill himself. The man's father, Joel Gavalas, has sued Alphabet for wrongful death and product liability. This is the latest in a growing series of legal actions against AI developers that have drawn attention to the mental health dangers of chatbots.

Alphabet's Legal Defense

"AI is sending people on real-world missions which risk mass casualty events," said the family's attorney Jay Edelson, in a media interview conducted after the lawsuit was filed. "He (Jonathan) believed that Gemini was sentient."

For its part, Alphabet said in a written statement that it sends its "deepest sympathies to Mr. Gavalas' family" and is reviewing the claims in the lawsuit. It said Gemini is "designed to not encourage real-world violence or suggest self-harm" and that the company works closely with medical and mental health professionals to develop safeguards.

"Our models generally perform well in these types of challenging conversations and we devote significant resources to this, but unfortunately AI models are not perfect," reads the company's statement.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Hazardous Substances in the Lower Duwamish": Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Notches Up Despite Lawsuit
data.description:
It is not every day that aerospace stock Boeing (BA) finds itself on the bad end of a lawsuit related to environmental practices. That was just what happened recently, though, as the federal government took aim at Boeing, and a group of others, over the discovery of "…hazardous substances in the Lower Duwamish." Oddly, investors were much less afraid of this than might be expected, as shares gained nearly 1.5% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The United States government, backed up by the state of Washington, took aim at Boeing, as well as the City of Seattle, King County, and several other unnamed entities measured in the dozens. The lawsuit filed focused on issues in the Lower Duwamish Waterway Superfund Site, and the cleanup costs the governments incurred as a result.

The governments involved want a court to hold the various defendants liable for the costs as part of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act as well as Washington's own Model Toxics Control Act. The area has been "…Seattle's major industrial corridor since the early 1900s," which makes for a lot of unpleasant matter slipping into the area. The EPA found 41 different substances in the area, from arsenic to dioxins, among others.

The Starliner Debacle Continues

Further, new reports emerged about the Starliner debacle, and how issues of culture actually impacted Boeing's ability to safely get its astronauts home. The formal declaration of a "Type A mishap" did not help matters, and further reports that "leadership accountability" would follow as NASA itself planned to work with Boeing on "corrective actions."

Reports detailed "communication breakdowns" and "unprofessional behavior" during the event, as Boeing and NASA experienced "disputes" on how to bring the crew back home, safely. There were also descriptions of "…strained decision-making dynamics and leadership failures…" involved as well, which is a serious problem for an operation that is departing the planet.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 13 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 37.36% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $278.17 per share implies 22.8% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Trump Formally Nominates Warsh to Succeed Powell as Confirmation Uncertainty Looms
data.description:
President Trump has formally nominated Kevin Warsh to replace Fed Chair Jerome Powell when his term ends on May 15, the White House said in a statement on Wednesday. Trump also submitted a nomination for Warsh to serve a 14-year term as a Fed governor.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If confirmed by the Senate, Warsh will serve a four-year term as Chair. However, Republican senators Thom Tillis and Lisa Murkowski have said that they will block Warsh's nomination until the Justice Department's investigation into Powell is resolved.

Trump's Chair Nominee Faces Senate Pushback

In January, Powell disclosed that he faced an investigation over the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. He claimed that the probe served as a pretext for the Fed setting interest rates independently instead of adhering to Trump's demands for lower rates.

Tillis echoed Powell's claim and emphasized that "No Republican purporting to care about Fed independence should agree to move forward with this nomination." Still, the Senate may be able to override Tillis and Murkowski's opposition before the end of Powell's term.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Jensen Huang Says It 'Might Be the Last Time' Nvidia (NVDA) Invests in OpenAI
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said that the chipmaker's recent $30 billion investment in OpenAI may be its final investment in the artificial intelligence startup before it potentially goes public later this year. Speaking at the Morgan Stanley (MS) Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Huang explained that Nvidia had previously discussed the possibility of investing as much as $100 billion in OpenAI as part of an infrastructure partnership. However, he noted that such a large investment is now unlikely, mostly because OpenAI is expected to pursue an IPO in the near future.

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 03/04/2026
data.description:
Investors seeking reliable income can look to long-established dividend aristocrats to supply a substantial share of their portfolio cash flows with consistent distributions through 2026. These include Hormel Foods (HRL), Kimberly-Clark (KMB), and Stanley Black & Decker (SWK), identified using the TipRanks Dividend Aristocrat tool.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock currently has a dividend payout ratio of at least 100%, meaning that each company recently distributed at least their entire net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. Hormel Foods (HRL) — This is an American packaged foods giant that develops, processes, and distributes various meat, nuts, and food products to domestic and international retail, foodservice, deli, and commercial customers. The Minnesota-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 170.95%. HRL paid 29 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.68%. Its shares currently have a Moderate Buy consensus rating from the two top Wall Street analysts covering the stock, with an average price target of $28, which implies about 15% upside.
  2. Kimberly-Clark (KMB) — This American corporation makes personal care and consumer tissue products such as disposable diapers, paper towels, and napkins that are sold across the world. The Texas-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 133.86%. It paid $1.28 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.59%. KMB stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 10 Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $124.89, implying about 20% upside.
  3. Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) — This U.S. manufacturer makes tools and accessories for both domestic and commercial usage. This includes items such as corded and cordless electric power tools, hydraulic equipment, and industrial coating equipment. The Connecticut-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 141.33%. The firm paid 83 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 3.93%. SWK stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 11 Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $89.90, which implies about 12% upside.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Dividend aristocrats within the S&P 500 index (SPX) are a small group of companies distinguished by increasing their dividends every year for at least 25 straight years, not just holding payouts flat. This characteristic tends to appeal to investors who prioritize steadily growing income streams rather than reacting to short-term share price swings. 

Newly created spin-off firms can sometimes join this club if they are viewed as having effectively continued their former parent company's long-running record of consistent dividend growth.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 3/4/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are rallying on Wednesday as investors digest two positive economic data points.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ADP reported that private employers added 63,000 jobs in February, the most since July 2025 and above the consensus estimate of 50,000. However, most of the growth was attributed to the education/health services and construction sectors, which added a combined 77,000 jobs. No other sector delivered more than 11,000 jobs, while the professional/business services sector lost 30,000 jobs.

ISM Services PMI Jumps to 3-Year High

Meanwhile, the ISM Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 56.1 in February, marking the highest level since July 2022 and above the estimate of 53.8. The index measures the health of the U.S. services sector by tracking new orders, employment, business activity, and supplier deliveries. A reading above 50 indicates expansion, while a reading below 50 points to contraction.

Every component of the index expanded for the first time since March 2021, while prices paid fell to 63.0, the lowest reading since March 2025 and signaling easing inflation.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Oil Could Skyrocket to $100, Warns Goldman Sachs
data.description:
The price for a barrel of Brent crude oil (BZ) has risen by 10% over the past week, although Goldman Sachs warns that extended disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz could send prices even higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The strait handles 20% of global oil flows and has been operating at roughly 15% capacity since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran over the weekend. Goldman's worst-case scenario is for the passage to continue operating at a low volume for five weeks, which could send prices above $100. Another factor that may increase prices is damage to oil infrastructure.

Oil Prices Could Spike Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions

Goldman's base case is for the strait to operate at 15% capacity for five more days, followed by a month-long recovery to normal volumes. Earlier this week, the firm raised its second-quarter Brent crude oil price target by $10 to $76. The firm also lifted its fourth-quarter estimate to $66.

Earlier this week, JPMorgan warned that oil prices could surge to $120 in the event of a prolonged U.S.-Iran conflict, adding that Gulf producers could only maintain normal production for 25 days in the event that the Strait of Hormuz is completely blocked.
data.author: Eddie Pan
